title: malus × sublobata
intro: Malus × sublobata, also known as the crabapple, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Malus domestica and Malus baccata. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Malus × sublobata include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which help to boost the immune system and fight off infections. Malus × sublobata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
